caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Payroll clerks absent from supplied 5412 proxy.
inputs.pi_moat: Platform evidence is not local-bureau evidence.

Validator: ADP's AI deployment supports capability at a scale platform, not the selected 55% current effective adoption rate across NAICS 541214; adoption timing remains a low-confidence platform-to-local-bureau extrapolation.

Validator: Public-platform filings do not substantiate the exact local consolidator count or 4.0x/4.5x payroll-bureau EBITDA multiples; B and M remain explicitly low-confidence estimates without local transaction evidence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $98,054 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541214); class <5: 2,110 firms, avg revenue/firm $581,493 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $91,004 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 662 firms, avg revenue/firm $919,816 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $143,951 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 480 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,588,508 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $248,602 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 741 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,599,022 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $719,747 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 297 firms, avg revenue/firm $13,560,636 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,122,240 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 194 firms, avg revenue/firm $89,119,103 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,947,140 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 297

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Processing and support automate heavily; supplied CPA proxy constrains weights.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541214 ($22,102,632,622) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541214 ($27,322,843,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

inputs.pi_moat: Platform evidence is not local-bureau evidence.

Validator: ADP's AI deployment supports capability at a scale platform, not the selected 55% current effective adoption rate across NAICS 541214; adoption timing remains a low-confidence platform-to-local-bureau extrapolation.

Validator: Public-platform filings do not substantiate the exact local consolidator count or 4.0x/4.5x payroll-bureau EBITDA multiples; B and M remain explicitly low-confidence estimates without local transaction evidence.
source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact mechanics, identities, hashes, deterministic equality, and arithmetic pass. Both registered sources are accessible and support the platform, regulatory, recurring-revenue, and AI-capability direction, while industrywide adoption timing and local-bureau buyability/multiples remain disclosed low-confidence extrapolations.
